Distress_Evidence: Long-running owner; aging operator; no succession evident (Yelp, county records)
Real_Estate_Upside: W 51st corridor seeing new retail; near Creek Turnpike interchange
Investment_Thesis: Aging owner in appreciating SW Tulsa corridor — motivated exit likely within 3 years.
Risks_Red_Flags: Lease vs own unclear; verify title before approach

Owner_Name_If_Known: Carolyn Sue Ashley (widow) + heirs; Ty Hogan (manager)
Distress_Evidence: Founder Roy Ashley died Nov 2017. Widow + 4 daughters operating with no succession plan. Adjacent Admiral Flea Market closed 2019 due to owner health/retirement, eroding corridor traffic. Estate-run operations are classic motivated-exit scenarios. (Source: Reynolds Funeral Homes obit; Route 66 News; Yelp Feb 2026)
Real_Estate_Upside: Route 66 alignment (E Admiral Place); near I-244/US-412; east Tulsa redevelopment pressure; 65k sqft footprint with rezoning/mixed-use potential
Hispanic_Population_Context: ZIP 74115 — significant and growing Hispanic population; same trade area as El Bazaar with 100+ Hispanic vendors on the Garnett corridor
Investment_Thesis: Post-founder estate market on Route 66 corridor — heirs lack operational passion and a motivated exit is imminent.
Risks_Red_Flags: Family may resist external sale while still operating; verify lease vs. own status
Source: Yelp / Reynolds Funeral Homes obit / Route 66 News
Notes: Direct outreach to Sue Ashley or Ty Hogan recommended

Distress_Evidence: Single-owner LLC with no apparent succession plan. Leased (not owned) property — lease expiration terms unknown and potentially unfavorable. No social media activity cadence; D&B classifies under 'Other Miscellaneous Retailers' — lightly formalized operation. (Source: D&B; FindTheMarkets; Yelp Feb 2026)
Real_Estate_Upside: S Garnett Rd at US-64/Hwy 412 interchange; high new retail/residential development pressure; Supermercados Morelos anchor next door confirms land value
Hispanic_Population_Context: ZIP 74129 — among highest-density Hispanic census tracts in Tulsa County. Explicitly serves Mexican/Central American vendors: piñatas, quinceañera goods, saddles, Spanish-language food vendors
Investment_Thesis: Aging sole-operator running Tulsa's densest Hispanic bazaar at a highway interchange — lease expiration creates a forced conversation.
Risks_Red_Flags: Leased not owned; must verify lease timeline with owner before approach
Notes: Direct outreach to Juvenal Saldivar; ask about retirement timeline and lease expiration date

Full_Address: 2233 N Prairie Creek Rd (relocated from 9334 E RL Thornton Fwy)
Owner_Name_If_Known: Elias Ramirez (manager on record, did not respond to press per KERA News Dec 2024)
Distress_Evidence: Dallas Board of Adjustments approved Home Depot takeover of original Thornton/Buckner site Oct 2024. Management told vendors to vacate by March 31, 2025. Mgmt statement: 'We tried to obtain an extension but we could not.' Vendor advocate Juanita Arevalo: 'It's going to be a financial hardship.' 20+ year operation rebuilding foot traffic at inferior new location. (Source: KERA News Dec 6 2024)
Real_Estate_Upside: New Prairie Creek Rd site in dense SE Dallas/Mesquite commercial corridor; original site validated by national retail (Home Depot) absorbing it
Hispanic_Population_Context: SE Dallas 75227/75228 is majority Hispanic; market serves small Latino-owned businesses as primary affordable retail destination per KERA reporting
Investment_Thesis: Forced-displaced 20-year bazaar with proven vendor base now fighting for survival at inferior new location — acquisition window before operator stabilizes or folds.
Risks_Red_Flags: Dallas is not Tulsa metro; operator may recover if new location builds traffic; verify current operator situation
Notes: Forced relocation distress — approach management about acquisition of operation/goodwill

Size_Booths_Acres: 151,505 sqft building / 305,364 sqft (7 acres) land / 230 tenants
Owner_Name_If_Known: Not publicly named; LoopNet listing broker contact required
Distress_Evidence: $20M LoopNet listing pulled from active advertising — sale fell through, price cut pending, or owner withdrew. Built 1953 (70-yr structure). Deferred maintenance. Delisted = motivated seller who has not found a buyer. (Source: LoopNet; hhb.shop; Yelp Feb 2026)
Real_Estate_Upside: 7 acres on Harry Hines Blvd adjacent to Dallas Medical District, I-35E, Love Field. Land value exceeds bazaar income value. Mixed-use/hospital development pressure. ~10.15% cap rate at $20M ask; $1.97M NOI
Hispanic_Population_Context: 75220/75229 — dense Hispanic population; 300+ shops including quinceañera dresses, western wear, authentic Mexican food; spine of Dallas's Mexican-American commercial corridor
Investment_Thesis: 7-acre highway-fronting land play in the heart of Dallas's Latino corridor — bazaar income validates the land; redevelopment exits the position.
Risks_Red_Flags: $20M ask requires significant capital; confirm listing status and broker contact; Dallas not Tulsa metro
Notes: Contact LoopNet listing broker directly; confirm if property is still available post-delisting

Distress_Evidence: Shut down 2020–Sep 2023 (3 years) for city code violations (electrical, planning, zoning). Owner filed lawsuit against City of El Paso during closure. Reopened Sep 30 2023 only after meeting compliance conditions. Preceded by 2019 fire. 3-year forced closure + costly legal battle + ongoing regulatory burden = financially/emotionally fatigued owner. (Source: KFOX14; CBS4 Local)
Real_Estate_Upside: 9 acres on US-62/180 (Alameda Ave) — primary east-west highway connecting to I-10. El Paso's Lower Valley is one of the fastest-growing US-Mexico border corridors. Nearshoring/border trade driving industrial and logistics development on this corridor.
Hispanic_Population_Context: ZIP 79907 (Lower Valley) is 95%+ Hispanic; Bronco Swap Meet operating since 1985 with 300+ vendors and 500+ dependent families — deeply embedded in the Hispanic economy
Investment_Thesis: Battle-worn owner of a 9-acre highway site who just survived 3 years of city enforcement hell — Sandoval is a motivated seller waiting for the right conversation.
Risks_Red_Flags: El Paso is well outside Tulsa metro; regulatory history requires due diligence on current compliance status; verify permit standing before approach
Notes: Direct outreach to Eddie Sandoval / Sandoval Capital Inc. — reference the reopening hardship to open the conversation
